original_hypothesis.title: EntityTrading listNFTForSale Resets Nuke Maturity Timer via Transfer Hook
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When listing an NFT for sale via EntityTrading.listNFTForSale, the token is transferred from owner to EntityTrading contract. This triggers _beforeTokenTransfer which resets lastTokenTransferredTimestamp to block.timestamp. When the listing is cancelled (cancelListing), the token transfers back, resetting the timestamp again. Any user who lists and cancels a token loses their accumulated nuke maturity progress (minimumDaysHeld = 3 days). Buying also resets the timer for the new owner.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Users approaching the 3-day nuke eligibility threshold who interact with EntityTrading unknowingly reset their timer. This creates an unexpected interaction between the trading and nuking systems that could trap users in a cycle of delayed nuke eligibility, reducing the expected economic utility of their tokens.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (from != to) { lastTokenTransferredTimestamp[firstTokenId] = block.timestamp; } fires on every transfer including listing/delisting

preconditions: User has been holding token approaching minimumDaysHeld threshold
User lists token on EntityTrading then cancels

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: listNFTForSale triggers transferFrom(user, entityTrading) → _beforeTokenTransfer resets lastTokenTransferredTimestamp; cancelListing triggers transferFrom(entityTrading, user) → resets again
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Listing + cancelling resets 3-day maturity timer, delaying nuke eligibility by up to 3 days each cycle
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Users lose time-value of money from delayed nuke payouts; may miss optimal nuke timing

economic_questions: How many users would realistically list tokens near nuke eligibility?
What is the economic cost of a 3-day delay in nuke payout?
Is there a griefing vector where someone forces another's timer reset?
validation_requirements: Confirm lastTokenTransferredTimestamp resets on list and cancel
Calculate time-value-of-money impact for delayed nuke
Verify no third party can force listing/transfer

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: N/A - no direct profit; time delay impacts user
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Each list/cancel cycle adds up to 3 days delay

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Unexpected UX issue affecting nuke timing strategy

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Users who list/cancel tokens on EntityTrading
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Up to 3 days additional wait for nuke eligibility

validation_result.mechanism: transfer hook maturity timer reset on listing/delisting
validation_result.impact_summary: Listing and cancelling an NFT on EntityTrading resets the lastTokenTransferredTimestamp twice, causing users to lose up to 3 days of accumulated nuke maturity progress per list/cancel cycle.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis is correct. When a user lists an NFT via EntityTrading.listNFTForSale, the token is transferred from the user to the EntityTrading contract. This triggers TraitForgeNft._beforeTokenTransfer, which unconditionally resets lastTokenTransferredTimestamp[tokenId] to block.timestamp (since from != to). When the user cancels the listing via cancelListing, the token is transferred back, triggering the hook again and resetting the timestamp a second time. Since NukeFund.canTokenBeNuked checks `block.timestamp - lastTokenTransferredTimestamp >= minimumDaysHeld` (3 days), any listing/cancellation cycle completely resets the user's nuke maturity progress. The _beforeTokenTransfer hook does not distinguish between 'real' user-to-user transfers and 'operational' transfers to/from the EntityTrading escrow contract.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: All code paths are verified in the source. The transfer hook at TraitForgeNft.sol:283-286 fires on every transfer where from != to. EntityTrading.listNFTForSale at line ~52 calls transferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), tokenId). EntityTrading.cancelListing at line ~82 calls transferFrom(address(this), msg.sender, tokenId). NukeFund.canTokenBeNuked at lines ~146-153 reads getTokenLastTransferredTimestamp to determine maturity.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: The hook resets lastTokenTransferredTimestamp on every transfer where from != to, including transfers to/from the EntityTrading escrow contract during listing and cancellation. This destroys nuke maturity progress.
Transfer from user to EntityTrading triggers _beforeTokenTransfer, resetting lastTokenTransferredTimestamp to block.timestamp.
Transfer from EntityTrading back to user triggers _beforeTokenTransfer again, resetting lastTokenTransferredTimestamp to block.timestamp a second time.

This function uses lastTokenTransferredTimestamp to determine nuke eligibility. Since listing/cancelling resets this timestamp, users must wait the full minimumDaysHeld (3 days) again after any trading interaction.

calling_sequence: 9. User attempts NukeFund.nuke(tokenId), which calls canTokenBeNuked(tokenId)
10. canTokenBeNuked computes block.timestamp - lastTokenTransferredTimestamp < minimumDaysHeld → returns false
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The transfer hook in _beforeTokenTransfer (TraitForgeNft.sol:285) fires unconditionally for all transfers where from != to. Since EntityTrading.listNFTForSale (line ~52) transfers from user to contract and cancelListing (line ~84) transfers from contract to user, both operations reset the maturity timer. The canTokenBeNuked function (NukeFund.sol:148-150) reads this timestamp to enforce the 3-day hold requirement. There is no exemption for transfers to/from the EntityTrading contract.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment configuration is relevant. The defect exists in the code logic regardless of deployment parameters.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: User holds token for 2.9 days (close to 3-day nuke eligibility). User decides to list on EntityTrading, then changes mind and cancels. Both operations trigger _beforeTokenTransfer, resetting lastTokenTransferredTimestamp to block.timestamp. User now must wait another full 3 days before being able to nuke. For buyNFT, the buyer receives a token with a freshly reset maturity timer regardless of how long the seller held it. This is not an 'attack' per se but a genuine code defect where the trading system's escrow mechanism interferes with the nuking system's maturity tracking.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Users who list and cancel NFTs on EntityTrading lose up to 3 days of accumulated nuke maturity. Users who buy NFTs always start with a fresh maturity timer. This creates an unexpected penalty for using the trading system and may trap users in repeated cycles of delayed nuke eligibility. The economic impact is the time-value-of-money from delayed nuke payouts and potentially missed optimal nuke timing windows when the NukeFund balance is high.
recommended_mitigations: Exempt EntityTrading contract address from the lastTokenTransferredTimestamp reset in _beforeTokenTransfer by adding a check: if (from != to && from != address(entityTradingContract) && to != address(entityTradingContract))
Alternatively, store the original lastTokenTransferredTimestamp before listing and restore it upon cancellation in EntityTrading
Consider implementing a separate 'maturity-preserving' transfer function for the trading contract escrow mechanism
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy all contracts. 2. Mint a token. 3. Advance time by 2.9 days. 4. Verify canTokenBeNuked returns false but is close to true. 5. Approve EntityTrading, call listNFTForSale. 6. Check lastTokenTransferredTimestamp - it should be reset to current block.timestamp. 7. Call cancelListing. 8. Check lastTokenTransferredTimestamp - reset again. 9. Verify canTokenBeNuked returns false and requires another full 3-day wait. 10. Advance time by 3 days, verify canTokenBeNuked returns true.
uncertainties: Whether this was an intentional design choice to reset maturity on any transfer (including trading), or an unintended consequence of the blanket transfer hook
additional_testing_needed: Test the interaction between EntityForging listing and nuke maturity (forging listings don't transfer the token, so may not be affected)
Test buyNFT path to confirm buyer also gets a reset timer
Quantify the economic impact of delayed nuke payouts based on typical NukeFund balance
